h2h_summary: Netherlands lead the all-time head-to-head with 11 wins to Sweden's 8, though in the modern era (post-1974) the Dutch have the upper hand with 3 wins and 1 draw in competitive matches.

notable_results: 2-0 Netherlands win - FIFA World Cup Qualifier - 2017
0-0 draw (Sweden win on penalties 5-4) - UEFA Euro 2004 Quarter-Final - 2004
0-0 draw - FIFA World Cup Group Stage - 1974

team_style_home: Netherlands under Koeman deploy a possession-based 4-3-3 that shifts to 4-2-3-1, building from the back with technically gifted defenders, using a high defensive line, line-breaking passes, and wide full-backs to stretch opponents.
team_style_away: Sweden under Graham Potter use a pragmatic, counter-attacking 3-4-3 that sacrifices possession for rapid transitions, relying on the explosive pace of Gyokeres and Elanga to punish defensive lines in behind.
wc_history_home: Netherlands are three-time World Cup runners-up (1974, 1978, 2010) and have reached the semi-finals or better in five tournaments, making them one of football's most decorated nations never to have won the trophy.
wc_history_away: Sweden's best World Cup finish was runners-up at home in 1958, and they claimed third place in 1994; their 13th World Cup appearance in 2026 comes after missing the 2022 tournament entirely.
group_context: Group F features Netherlands (FIFA rank 7th), Japan (18th), Sweden (38th), and Tunisia (45th), with Netherlands as clear favourites to top the group; the Netherlands vs Sweden match on Matchday 2 is effectively a pivotal battle for first place, as both teams are expected to beat Tunisia and the winner will likely secure top spot.
strengths_home: Elite technical midfield with de Jong, Gravenberch, and Simons controlling tempo
Settled, experienced defensive unit led by Van Dijk with Premier League-quality depth
Clinical attacking options in Gakpo, Depay, and Kluivert capable of unlocking deep blocks
strengths_away: Devastating counter-attacking pace with Gyokeres and Elanga among Europe's most dangerous transition threats
Gyokeres in lethal form after 21+ goals in 2025-26 and match-winning playoff performances
Compact defensive structure under Potter that concedes few chances and is hard to break down
